data_description.camera_entity: Camera to capture snapshots from. After adding it, use 'Add trigger' on the integration page to create one or more timelapse triggers for it.

abort.already_configured: This camera is already set up. Add another trigger to the existing entry instead.

data_description.name: Used for the device name and as the name placeholder in filenames.
data_description.trigger_mode: Manual: start and stop with the switch or services. Daily time window: capture between two times every day. Entity state watch: capture while another entity is in chosen states.
data_description.capture_mode: Time interval: a snapshot every N seconds. Entity value change: a snapshot each time a numeric entity moves by a chosen step, for example one frame per 3D-printer layer.
data_description.output_dir: Folder for finished videos, created automatically if it does not exist (for example /media/timelapses). Leave empty for the default media folder. Anything under /media shows up in the Media Browser, including NAS shares added via Settings > System > Storage. Paths outside a media folder must be listed in allowlist_external_dirs.
data_description.filename_pattern: Pattern for the video filename. Wrap the placeholders name, timestamp or entry_id in curly braces to insert them, as in the default value.
data_description.keep_frames: Keep the individual snapshot frames on disk after the video is rendered.

data_description.name: Used for the device name and as the name placeholder in filenames.
data_description.trigger_mode: Manual: start and stop with the switch or services. Daily time window: capture between two times every day. Entity state watch: capture while another entity is in chosen states.
data_description.capture_mode: Time interval: a snapshot every N seconds. Entity value change: a snapshot each time a numeric entity moves by a chosen step, for example one frame per 3D-printer layer.
data_description.output_dir: Folder for finished videos, created automatically if it does not exist (for example /media/timelapses). Leave empty for the default media folder. Anything under /media shows up in the Media Browser, including NAS shares added via Settings > System > Storage. Paths outside a media folder must be listed in allowlist_external_dirs.
data_description.filename_pattern: Pattern for the video filename. Wrap the placeholders name, timestamp or entry_id in curly braces to insert them, as in the default value.
data_description.keep_frames: Keep the individual snapshot frames on disk after the video is rendered.

data_description.schedule_start: Capture starts at this time every day.
data_description.schedule_end: Capture stops and the video is rendered at this time. Overnight windows (for example 22:00 to 06:00) are supported.

data_description.watch_entity: The entity that drives the capture, for example a 3D printer status sensor or a motion sensor. You pick its active states in the next step.

data_description.watch_states: Capture runs while the entity is in any of these states. When it leaves them (including becoming unavailable), the capture stops and the video is rendered.

data_description.value_entity: Numeric entity that paces the frames, for example a 3D printer's current layer sensor, an energy meter, or a distance sensor.
data_description.value_delta: A frame is captured each time the value moves by at least this amount since the last frame. Use 1 for one frame per layer.
data_description.value_direction: Which way the value must move to capture. With increase or decrease, movement the other way re-baselines silently (a layer counter resetting for a new print just starts counting again).

error.path_not_allowed: This path is not absolute or not allowed. Add it to allowlist_external_dirs or use a media folder.
error.schedule_start_equals_end: Start and end time must be different.
error.delta_positive: The step must be greater than zero.

start.name: Start capture
start.description: Starts a timelapse capture session for a trigger.

stop.name: Stop capture
stop.description: Stops the capture session and renders the timelapse video.

render.name: Render video
render.description: Render the video after stopping. If disabled, frames are discarded unless 'keep frames' is enabled.

render.name: Render again
render.description: Renders the most recent retained frame set again (kept frames or a failed render).

cancel.name: Cancel capture
cancel.description: Aborts the capture session and discards its frames without rendering.

device_not_found.message: No Auto Time Lapse trigger found for device {device_id}.

entry_not_loaded.message: Auto Time Lapse entry {title} is not loaded.

no_frames.message: There are no retained frames to render. Capture a session first, or enable 'keep frames'.
